change_comments.message: pretty good, mainly move version definition to dependencies pom and a few quesions...
Just one comment left regarding SQL dialect used in testcode, see below

As far as I've seen, the latest stage of your commit could not publish the cloud events into one of the Kafka topic by raising javax.management.InstanceAlreadyExistsException for the bean of KafkaTemplate which is for CloudEvent.

You can have a look at cps-and-ncmp docker logs from this link:

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/cps-review-verification-maven-master/5029/consoleText


The beginning of the log:

{"logTimeStamp":"2023-09-04T15:25:47.501Z","logLevel":"WARN","principalId":"cpsuser","serviceName":"cps-application","message":"Error registering AppInfo mbean","processId":"1","threadName":"org.springframework.kafka.KafkaListenerEndpointContainer#1-0-C-1","class":"o.a.kafka.common.utils.AppInfoParser","exception":"\njavax.management.InstanceAlreadyExistsException:
That's a good spot. Having a look into it. Not sure what is going on yet
This seems common across the board. It's not the issue here.The Kafka flow can't be verified for some reason here.
I'm looking at this to see what's going on in the verification flow, will let you know if I manage to sort it out.

In order to sort this out, there is a commit rose up. Once that get merged, you may rebase this patchset. Sorry for inconvenience.

135926: Verify kafka headers by header name | https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/135926
I see I never posted below comment but they are non-blocking
not suer if the duplication is blocking. If it builds I am happy if you organise them properly in a separate commit...
+1 on condition that the outstanding comments are handled as part of separate ticket.
